2603.23902.title: Knowledge-Refined Dual Context-Aware Network for Partially Relevant Video Retrieval
2603.23902.paper_summary: Retrieving partially relevant segments from untrimmed videos remains difficult due to two persistent challenges: the mismatch in information density between text and video segments, and limited attention mechanisms that overlook semantic focus and event correlations. We present KDC-Net, a Knowledge-Refined Dual Context-Aware Network that tackles these issues from both textual and visual perspectives. On the text side, a Hierarchical Semantic Aggregation module captures and adaptively fuses multi-scale phrase cues to enrich query semantics. On the video side, a Dynamic Temporal Attention mechanism employs relative positional encoding and adaptive temporal windows to highlight key events with local temporal coherence. Additionally, a dynamic CLIP-based distillation strategy, enhanced with temporal-continuity-aware refinement, ensures segment-aware and objective-aligned knowledge transfer. Experiments on PRVR benchmarks show that KDC-Net consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, especially under low moment-to-video ratios.
2603.23902.paper_summary_zh: 從未修剪的視頻中檢索部分相關片段仍然困難，這主要是由於兩個持續存在的挑戰：文本和視頻片段之間的信息密度不匹配，以及有限的注意機制忽略了語義焦點和事件相關性。我們提出了 KDC-Net，一個知識精煉的雙重上下文感知網絡，從文本和視覺兩個角度解決這些問題。在文本方面，層次語義聚合模塊捕捉並自適應融合多尺度短語線索，以豐富查詢語義。在視頻方面，動態時間注意機制採用相對位置編碼和自適應時間窗口，突出具有局部時間一致性的關鍵事件。此外，增強了時間連續性感知精煉的動態 CLIP 基於蒸餾策略，確保片段感知和目標對齊的知識轉移。在 PRVR 基準上的實驗顯示，KDC-Net 一直優於最先進的方法，特別是在低時刻與視頻比率下。
2603.23902.author: Junkai Yang et.al.
2603.23902.authors: Junkai Yang, Qirui Wang, Yaoqing Jin, Shuai Ma, Minghan Xu, Shanmin Pang
